input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI data binding issue where labels appear blank despite the ViewModel having values. This is a common issue - let me diagnose it.

The most likely cause: the `BindingContext` is never set on the page. The XAML shows bindings but there's no indication that the page's `BindingContext` is assigned to an instance of the ViewModel.

Another possible issue: the ViewModel properties might not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the user says the properties "have values" — if they're set before the binding is established, INPC wouldn't matter. But if BindingContext is set and then properties are updated later without INPC, that could also cause blank labels.
 
That's really the core issue here - without setting the BindingContext, the bindings have nothing to connect to, so the labels stay empty even though the ViewModel has the data.
